title: Neoalsomitra pubescens
uses: Neoalsomitra pubescens is reported to have potential medicinal uses in traditional medicine, including its application in treating fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. taxonomy: Neoalsomitra pubescens is classified as a species of plant in the family Cucurbitaceae. This family includes a wide range of plants commonly known as gourds and melons. Neoalsomitra pubescens is further classified as a genus within the tribe Aechmeae. The classification is part of the broader plant taxonomy, which helps to organize and understand the diversity of plant species. side_effects: Neoalsomitra pubescens is considered to be toxic and should be handled with caution. Ingestion of its parts may cause gastrointestinal irritation, vomiting, and diarrhea. intro: Neoalsomitra pubescens, also known as the hairy cordia vine or hairy alismite, is a plant species belonging to the family Capparaceae. The health benefits of Neoalsomitra pubescens include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
